storyTitle: As Operations Manager, I want to perform module-level performance benchmarking and comparison to achieve identification of optimization opportunities across insurance operations

functional.title: Verify benchmark report displays all insurance modules ranked by response time
preconditions: User is logged in as Operations Manager with 'benchmark_reports' permission
At least 5 insurance modules (Claims, Underwriting, Policy Admin, Billing, Renewals) have performance data recorded in the production environment
Defined performance targets exist for each module in the system configuration (e.g., Claims target response time: 200ms, throughput: 500 req/s, error rate: <1%)
User is on the main Operations Dashboard page

steps.action: Click on 'Performance Benchmarking' in the left navigation menu under 'Reports' section
steps.expectedResult: The Performance Benchmarking page loads displaying a header 'Module-Level Performance Benchmarking', environment selector defaulting to 'Production', and a date range picker defaulting to the last 30 days

steps.action: Verify the default benchmark report table is displayed with all insurance modules listed
steps.expectedResult: A table is displayed with columns: 'Rank', 'Module Name', 'Avg Response Time (ms)', 'Response Time Target (ms)', 'Throughput (req/s)', 'Throughput Target (req/s)', 'Error Rate (%)', 'Error Rate Target (%)', and 'Status'. All 5 modules are listed and ranked from slowest to fastest by response time

steps.action: Click on the 'Rank By' dropdown and select 'Response Time' if not already selected
steps.expectedResult: Modules are sorted in descending order by average response time, with the slowest module ranked #1 at the top. Each module row shows a red indicator if response time exceeds target, green if within target

steps.action: Click on the column header 'Throughput (req/s)' to re-rank modules by throughput
steps.expectedResult: The table re-sorts with modules ranked by throughput in ascending order (lowest throughput first). Rank numbers update accordingly. Modules below throughput target show a red indicator

steps.action: Click on the column header 'Error Rate (%)' to re-rank modules by error rate
steps.expectedResult: The table re-sorts with modules ranked by error rate in descending order (highest error rate first). Modules exceeding the error rate target threshold display a red warning icon and the 'Status' column shows 'Exceeds Target'

steps.action: Verify the summary section at the top of the report shows aggregate statistics
steps.expectedResult: A summary panel displays: total modules benchmarked (5), number of modules exceeding at least one target threshold, overall average response time, overall average throughput, and overall average error rate across all modules

postconditions: Benchmark report remains displayed with the last selected ranking criteria (Error Rate)
No data has been modified; the report is read-only
User session remains active on the Performance Benchmarking page

functional.title: Verify benchmark report displays top operations within each module ranked by performance metrics
preconditions: User is logged in as Operations Manager with 'benchmark_reports' permission
The Claims module has at least 10 distinct operations with recorded performance data (e.g., 'Submit Claim', 'Approve Claim', 'Reject Claim', 'Search Claims', etc.)
Performance targets are defined at the operation level for the Claims module
User is on the Performance Benchmarking page with the default report loaded

steps.action: In the benchmark report table, click on the 'Claims' module row to expand or drill down into its operations
steps.expectedResult: A detailed sub-table or drill-down view opens showing all operations within the Claims module, each with columns: 'Operation Name', 'Avg Response Time (ms)', 'Target Response Time (ms)', 'Throughput (req/s)', 'Target Throughput', 'Error Rate (%)', 'Target Error Rate (%)', and 'Variance from Target'

steps.action: Verify that operations are ranked by response time in descending order (slowest first) by default
steps.expectedResult: Operations are listed with the slowest operation at the top. For example, 'Search Claims' might show 850ms against a 300ms target, appearing first. Each operation shows a percentage variance from target (e.g., '+183%' in red text)

steps.action: Identify the top 5 operations listed and verify they include response time, throughput, and error rate data
steps.expectedResult: Each of the top 5 operations displays non-null values for all three metrics. Operations exceeding any target threshold are highlighted with a visual indicator (red background or warning icon)

steps.action: Click the 'Back to Module Overview' breadcrumb or back button
steps.expectedResult: User returns to the module-level benchmark report with all modules still ranked as previously configured

steps.action: Click on the 'Underwriting' module row to drill down into its operations
steps.expectedResult: The Underwriting operations sub-table loads showing all operations within Underwriting, ranked by response time descending, with the same column structure as the Claims drill-down

postconditions: User can navigate between module-level and operation-level views without data loss
All drill-down data is consistent with the selected date range and environment
Report state is preserved when navigating back to module overview

functional.title: Verify optimization recommendations are generated for operations exceeding target thresholds
preconditions: User is logged in as Operations Manager with 'benchmark_reports' permission

At least 3 operations across different modules have metrics exceeding their defined target thresholds (e.g., Claims 'Search Claims' response time 850ms vs 300ms target, Billing 'Generate Invoice' error rate 5% vs 1% target, Underwriting 'Risk Assessment' throughput 50 req/s vs 200 req/s target)
The optimization recommendation engine is configured and active
User is on the Performance Benchmarking page

steps.action: Click on the 'Optimization Recommendations' tab or section within the Performance Benchmarking page
steps.expectedResult: An 'Optimization Recommendations' panel loads displaying a list of recommendations, each tied to a specific operation that exceeds its target threshold. The list is sorted by estimated improvement impact (highest impact first)

steps.action: Review the first recommendation in the list for the operation with the highest impact
steps.expectedResult: The recommendation displays: 'Module Name', 'Operation Name', 'Current Metric Value', 'Target Value', 'Variance', 'Recommendation Description' (e.g., 'Optimize database query indexing for Claims Search'), and 'Estimated Improvement Impact' (e.g., 'Potential 60% response time reduction, saving ~330ms per request')

steps.action: Verify that each recommendation includes an estimated improvement impact with quantified metrics
steps.expectedResult: All listed recommendations show a quantified estimated impact, such as percentage improvement, absolute time savings, or throughput gain. No recommendation has a blank or 'N/A' impact field

steps.action: Click on the second recommendation to expand its details
steps.expectedResult: An expanded view shows additional details including: historical trend of the metric, when the threshold was first exceeded, severity classification (Critical/High/Medium/Low), and suggested action steps for remediation

steps.action: Verify that only operations exceeding their target thresholds have recommendations generated
steps.expectedResult: No recommendations are listed for operations that are within their target thresholds. The total count of recommendations matches the number of operations exceeding at least one target metric

steps.action: Click 'Export Recommendations' button in the top-right of the recommendations panel
steps.expectedResult: A file download dialog appears offering export in CSV or PDF format. The exported file contains all recommendations with their full details including module, operation, metrics, and estimated impact

postconditions: Optimization recommendations remain displayed and are not modified by viewing them
Exported file is saved to the user's default download location
Recommendations are timestamped with the report generation date and selected date range

functional.title: Verify benchmark comparisons across dev, staging, and production environments identify performance variances
preconditions: User is logged in as Operations Manager with 'benchmark_reports' permission and access to all three environments (dev, staging, production)
Performance data exists for the same set of modules and operations across dev, staging, and production environments for the same date range
At least one module shows a significant performance variance between environments (e.g., Claims module response time: dev=150ms, staging=300ms, production=850ms)
User is on the Performance Benchmarking page

steps.action: Locate the 'Environment Comparison' section or tab on the Performance Benchmarking page and click on it
steps.expectedResult: An environment comparison view loads showing a multi-environment selector with checkboxes for 'Development', 'Staging', and 'Production'. All three are checked by default

steps.action: Ensure all three environments (Development, Staging, Production) are selected and click 'Generate Comparison'
steps.expectedResult: A side-by-side comparison table loads showing each module with its performance metrics (response time, throughput, error rate) displayed in separate columns for each environment. A 'Variance' column highlights the difference between environments

steps.action: Review the Claims module row in the comparison table
steps.expectedResult: The Claims module row shows: Dev response time (150ms), Staging response time (300ms), Production response time (850ms). The variance column shows the percentage difference between the best and worst performing environments (e.g., '+467% variance between dev and production'). The production column is highlighted in red indicating it is the worst performer

steps.action: Click on the 'Claims' module row to drill down into operation-level environment comparison
steps.expectedResult: An operation-level comparison table loads showing each Claims operation with its metrics across all three environments. Operations with the largest environment-specific variances are highlighted and sorted to the top

steps.action: Deselect the 'Development' environment checkbox and click 'Update Comparison'
steps.expectedResult: The comparison table updates to show only Staging and Production columns. The variance column recalculates to show differences between only the two selected environments. The Development column is removed from the view

steps.action: Re-select 'Development' and verify the comparison chart/visualization is available
steps.expectedResult: A visual chart (bar chart or line chart) is displayed below the table showing a graphical comparison of each module's response time across all three environments, making it easy to visually identify environment-specific performance variances

postconditions: Environment comparison data is displayed accurately reflecting actual performance data from each environment
User can toggle between environments without page reload or data loss
The comparison view correctly identifies and highlights environment-specific variances with quantified differences

functional.title: Verify date range selection filters benchmark data correctly
preconditions: User is logged in as Operations Manager with 'benchmark_reports' permission
Performance data exists for at least the last 90 days across all modules
User is on the Performance Benchmarking page with the default 30-day date range loaded

steps.action: Click on the date range picker and select 'Last 7 Days' from the preset options
steps.expectedResult: The date range picker updates to show the last 7 days. The benchmark report table refreshes and displays metrics calculated only from data within the last 7 days. A loading indicator appears briefly during data refresh

steps.action: Note the response time values for the Claims module, then change the date range to 'Last 90 Days'
steps.expectedResult: The benchmark report refreshes with data from the last 90 days. The Claims module response time value may differ from the 7-day view, reflecting the broader data set. The date range displayed in the report header updates to show the 90-day range

steps.action: Click on the date range picker and select 'Custom Range'. Enter a start date of 2 months ago and an end date of 1 month ago, then click 'Apply'
steps.expectedResult: The benchmark report refreshes showing data only from the specified custom date range. The report header displays the exact custom date range selected. All module metrics reflect only the data within that specific window

steps.action: Navigate to the 'Optimization Recommendations' tab while the custom date range is still active
steps.expectedResult: Optimization recommendations are generated based on the custom date range data. The recommendations header shows the active date range filter. Recommendations may differ from the default 30-day view

steps.action: Navigate to the 'Environment Comparison' tab while the custom date range is still active
steps.expectedResult: Environment comparison data reflects the custom date range across all selected environments. The date range filter persists across all tabs/sections of the benchmarking page

postconditions: The selected custom date range persists across all sections of the Performance Benchmarking page
Data displayed is consistent with the selected date range filter
User can change the date range at any time without losing other filter selections (environment, sort order)

functional.title: Verify authentication and role-based access for Operations Manager accessing benchmark reports
preconditions: An Operations Manager user account exists with 'benchmark_reports' permission
A standard Agent user account exists without 'benchmark_reports' permission
Both accounts are active and have valid credentials
The Performance Benchmarking feature is enabled in the system

steps.action: Log in with the Operations Manager credentials (username: 'ops_manager@test.com', password: 'ValidPass123!')
steps.expectedResult: User is authenticated successfully and redirected to the Operations Dashboard. The left navigation menu displays the 'Performance Benchmarking' option under the 'Reports' section

steps.action: Click on 'Performance Benchmarking' in the navigation menu
steps.expectedResult: The Performance Benchmarking page loads successfully with full access to all features: module ranking table, optimization recommendations, environment comparison, date range picker, and export functionality

steps.action: Log out and log in with the standard Agent credentials (username: 'agent@test.com', password: 'AgentPass123!')
steps.expectedResult: Agent user is authenticated successfully and redirected to their dashboard. The left navigation menu does NOT display the 'Performance Benchmarking' option under 'Reports'

steps.action: Attempt to navigate directly to the Performance Benchmarking page by entering the URL '/reports/performance-benchmarking' in the browser address bar
steps.expectedResult: The system displays an 'Access Denied' or '403 Forbidden' message indicating the user does not have permission to access this feature. The user is not shown any benchmark data

postconditions: Operations Manager retains full access to benchmark reports upon subsequent logins
Agent user is not able to access benchmark data through any navigation path or direct URL
Access denial is logged in the system audit trail

functional.title: Verify benchmark report correctly compares actual performance against defined targets with status indicators
preconditions: User is logged in as Operations Manager on the Performance Benchmarking page
Defined targets are configured: Claims module - response time target: 300ms, throughput target: 500 req/s, error rate target: 1%
Actual Claims module performance: response time: 450ms, throughput: 480 req/s, error rate: 0.5%
At least one module meets all its targets and at least one module exceeds at least one target

steps.action: Locate the Claims module row in the benchmark report table
steps.expectedResult: Claims module row displays: Avg Response Time = 450ms (Target: 300ms), Throughput = 480 req/s (Target: 500 req/s), Error Rate = 0.5% (Target: 1%). Response Time shows red indicator (exceeds target by 50%), Throughput shows red indicator (below target by 4%), Error Rate shows green indicator (within target)

steps.action: Locate a module that meets all its targets (e.g., Renewals module with response time 180ms vs 250ms target, throughput 600 req/s vs 400 req/s target, error rate 0.3% vs 1% target)
steps.expectedResult: Renewals module row displays all metrics in green with green status indicators. The 'Status' column shows 'Within Target' or a green checkmark. No warning icons are displayed for this module

steps.action: Hover over the response time value for the Claims module
steps.expectedResult: A tooltip appears showing: 'Actual: 450ms | Target: 300ms | Variance: +150ms (+50%)'. The tooltip clearly indicates the metric is above the acceptable threshold

steps.action: Verify the overall status summary at the top of the report
steps.expectedResult: The summary shows the count of modules with status 'Exceeds Target' (at least 1) and 'Within Target' (at least 1). A pie chart or progress bar visualizes the proportion of modules meeting vs exceeding targets

postconditions: All target comparisons are mathematically accurate (actual vs target with correct variance calculation)
Color coding and status indicators are consistent across all modules
Target values displayed match the configured targets in the system settings

functional.title: Verify system correctly processes and returns benchmark data via API endpoint
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid API token for Operations Manager role
Performance data exists in the application database for at least 3 modules
API endpoint for benchmark reports is available at the expected URL (e.g., '/api/v1/benchmarks/modules')
Defined targets are configured in the system for all modules

steps.action: Send a GET request to '/api/v1/benchmarks/modules' with Authorization header containing a valid bearer token and query parameters: environment=production, dateFrom=2024-01-01, dateTo=2024-01-31
steps.expectedResult: API returns HTTP 200 OK with a JSON response body containing an array of module benchmark objects. Each object includes: moduleId, moduleName, avgResponseTime, responseTimeTarget, throughput, throughputTarget, errorRate, errorRateTarget, rank, and status fields

steps.action: Send a GET request to '/api/v1/benchmarks/modules/{moduleId}/operations' for a specific module to retrieve operation-level benchmarks
steps.expectedResult: API returns HTTP 200 OK with a JSON array of operation benchmark objects within the specified module. Each object includes: operationId, operationName, avgResponseTime, responseTimeTarget, throughput, throughputTarget, errorRate, errorRateTarget, and varianceFromTarget

steps.action: Send a GET request to '/api/v1/benchmarks/recommendations' with the same date range and environment parameters
steps.expectedResult: API returns HTTP 200 OK with a JSON array of optimization recommendation objects. Each includes: recommendationId, moduleId, operationId, currentValue, targetValue, variance, description, estimatedImpact, and severity

steps.action: Send a GET request to '/api/v1/benchmarks/environments/compare' with environments=dev,staging,production
steps.expectedResult: API returns HTTP 200 OK with a JSON response containing environment comparison data. Each module has metrics broken down by environment with calculated variance between environments

steps.action: Verify the response time of the API call is within SLA requirements
steps.expectedResult: The API response is returned within the defined SLA (e.g., under 3 seconds for standard report queries). Response headers include appropriate cache control and content-type headers

postconditions: All API responses contain valid JSON with correct data types for each field
API responses are consistent with the data displayed in the UI for the same parameters
No server errors (5xx) are returned for valid requests with proper authentication

functional.title: Verify data persistence - benchmark data is correctly stored and retrieved from the application database
preconditions: User is logged in as Operations Manager with 'benchmark_reports' permission
New performance data has been ingested into the system within the last hour for the Claims module
User is on the Performance Benchmarking page
Database connectivity is stable and operational

steps.action: Set the date range to include today's date and click 'Generate Report' or refresh the benchmark report
steps.expectedResult: The benchmark report loads and includes the most recently ingested performance data. The Claims module metrics reflect the latest data points including those ingested within the last hour

steps.action: Note the exact values displayed for the Claims module (response time, throughput, error rate) and the report generation timestamp
steps.expectedResult: Values are displayed with appropriate precision (e.g., response time to nearest millisecond, error rate to two decimal places). A 'Report Generated At' timestamp is shown indicating when the data was last calculated

steps.action: Navigate away from the Performance Benchmarking page to the main dashboard, then navigate back to the Performance Benchmarking page
steps.expectedResult: The benchmark report reloads with the same data values as previously observed (within the same date range). Data is consistently retrieved from the database without discrepancies

steps.action: Open the Performance Benchmarking page in a new browser tab simultaneously
steps.expectedResult: The second tab displays the same benchmark data as the first tab, confirming data consistency across concurrent sessions. Both tabs show identical metrics for the same date range and environment

postconditions: Benchmark data persists in the database and is retrievable across multiple sessions
Data integrity is maintained - no data corruption or loss between page loads
Concurrent access does not cause data inconsistencies

functional.title: Verify error handling for benchmark report generation with proper user feedback
preconditions: User is logged in as Operations Manager with 'benchmark_reports' permission
User is on the Performance Benchmarking page
System is operational with normal connectivity

steps.action: Select a date range where no performance data exists (e.g., a future date range: start date = tomorrow, end date = next week) and click 'Generate Report'
steps.expectedResult: The system displays an informational message such as 'No benchmark data available for the selected date range. Please select a different date range.' The report table shows an empty state with no module rows. No error or crash occurs

steps.action: Select a valid date range and select an environment that has no data (e.g., if a 'QA' environment option exists but has no data)
steps.expectedResult: The system displays a message indicating no data is available for the selected environment. The UI remains functional and the user can switch to another environment without issues

steps.action: Attempt to generate an environment comparison with only one environment selected
steps.expectedResult: The system either displays a validation message 'Please select at least two environments for comparison' or shows the single environment data with a note that comparison requires multiple environments

steps.action: Verify that the page handles a slow database response gracefully by observing the loading state during report generation
steps.expectedResult: A loading spinner or progress indicator is displayed while data is being fetched. The UI remains responsive and the user can cancel or navigate away. If the request times out, a user-friendly timeout message is displayed with a 'Retry' option

postconditions: System remains stable and functional after encountering empty data or error conditions
User is provided with clear, actionable feedback for all error scenarios
No unhandled exceptions or blank screens are displayed to the user

functional.title: Verify export functionality for benchmark reports in multiple formats
preconditions: User is logged in as Operations Manager with 'benchmark_reports' permission
A benchmark report is loaded on the Performance Benchmarking page with data for at least 5 modules
The date range is set to 'Last 30 Days' and environment is 'Production'

steps.action: Click the 'Export' button located in the top-right corner of the benchmark report
steps.expectedResult: A dropdown or modal appears with export format options: 'CSV', 'PDF', and optionally 'Excel'. Each option is clearly labeled

steps.action: Select 'CSV' from the export options
steps.expectedResult: A CSV file is downloaded with a descriptive filename (e.g., 'benchmark_report_production_2024-01-01_2024-01-31.csv'). The file contains headers matching the report columns and all module data rows with correct values

steps.action: Open the downloaded CSV file and verify its contents
steps.expectedResult: The CSV contains all modules with their rank, name, response time, throughput, error rate, targets, and status. Values match exactly what is displayed in the UI report. The file includes a header row with the date range and environment metadata

steps.action: Click 'Export' again and select 'PDF'
steps.expectedResult: A PDF file is downloaded with a formatted report including the benchmark table, summary statistics, and any charts/visualizations. The PDF includes a title, date range, environment, and generation timestamp

steps.action: Navigate to the Optimization Recommendations tab and click 'Export Recommendations'
steps.expectedResult: The recommendations are exported in the selected format, including all recommendation details: module, operation, current value, target, variance, description, and estimated impact

postconditions: Exported files are saved to the user's default download directory
Exported data is complete and matches the on-screen report exactly
Export functionality does not alter or affect the displayed report data

functional.title: Verify benchmark report refreshes with real-time or near-real-time data updates
preconditions: User is logged in as Operations Manager on the Performance Benchmarking page
The report is loaded with current data for the 'Production' environment
New performance data is being continuously ingested into the system
A manual or automatic refresh mechanism exists on the page

steps.action: Note the current 'Report Generated At' or 'Last Updated' timestamp displayed on the benchmark report
steps.expectedResult: A timestamp is visible showing when the report data was last calculated/refreshed (e.g., 'Last Updated: 2024-01-15 10:30:00 UTC')

steps.action: Wait for 5 minutes (or the configured refresh interval) and observe the report
steps.expectedResult: If auto-refresh is enabled, the report automatically updates with a new timestamp. If manual refresh is required, a 'Refresh' button or indicator shows that newer data is available

steps.action: Click the 'Refresh' button (if manual refresh) to update the report
steps.expectedResult: The report refreshes with updated metrics. The 'Last Updated' timestamp changes to the current time. A brief loading indicator appears during the refresh. Module rankings may change if new data affects the metrics

steps.action: Verify that the refreshed data reflects any changes in module performance
steps.expectedResult: If a module's performance has changed (e.g., response time improved or degraded), the updated values are reflected in the report. Rankings are recalculated based on the new data. Status indicators update accordingly

postconditions: Report displays the most current data available after refresh
Historical data is not lost or overwritten by the refresh - only the current view updates
The refresh mechanism does not cause data inconsistencies or partial updates

functional.title: Verify input validation for date range and filter parameters on benchmark page
preconditions: User is logged in as Operations Manager on the Performance Benchmarking page
The date range picker and environment selector are visible and functional

steps.action: In the custom date range picker, set the 'Start Date' to a date after the 'End Date' (e.g., Start: 2024-02-01, End: 2024-01-01) and click 'Apply'
steps.expectedResult: The system displays a validation error message: 'Start date must be before end date' or prevents the invalid selection. The report does not refresh with invalid parameters

steps.action: Set the date range to an extremely large range (e.g., 5 years) and click 'Apply'
steps.expectedResult: The system either processes the request with a warning about large data sets and potential slow performance, or limits the maximum date range with a message like 'Maximum date range is 1 year. Please narrow your selection.'

steps.action: Attempt to deselect all environments in the environment comparison view and click 'Generate Comparison'
steps.expectedResult: The system displays a validation message: 'Please select at least one environment' and prevents the report from generating with no environments selected

steps.action: Enter a valid date range and valid environment selection, then click 'Generate Report'
steps.expectedResult: The report generates successfully with no validation errors. All filters are applied correctly and the data reflects the selected parameters

postconditions: Invalid inputs are rejected with clear, user-friendly validation messages
The system does not crash or display errors for any combination of filter inputs
Valid inputs are processed correctly after correcting any validation errors

functional.title: Verify benchmark report UI elements render correctly and are interactive
preconditions: User is logged in as Operations Manager on the Performance Benchmarking page
Benchmark data is loaded for at least 5 modules in the Production environment
Browser is a supported modern browser (Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Safari latest versions)

steps.action: Verify the page layout includes: page title 'Module-Level Performance Benchmarking', date range picker, environment selector, benchmark table, summary panel, and navigation tabs for 'Module Rankings', 'Optimization Recommendations', and 'Environment Comparison'
steps.expectedResult: All listed UI elements are present and visible on the page. The layout is well-organized with clear section headers. No elements overlap or are cut off

steps.action: Resize the browser window to a tablet width (768px) and verify the page responsiveness
steps.expectedResult: The page layout adjusts responsively. The benchmark table may switch to a scrollable horizontal view or a card-based layout. All data remains visible and accessible. No content is hidden or inaccessible

steps.action: Click on each column header in the benchmark table to verify sorting functionality
steps.expectedResult: Each column header is clickable and sorts the table by that column. A sort direction indicator (arrow up/down) appears on the active sort column. Clicking the same column header toggles between ascending and descending sort

steps.action: Verify that color coding is consistent: green for within-target metrics, red/orange for exceeding-target metrics
steps.expectedResult: All metrics within target show green text or green background. All metrics exceeding target show red or orange text/background. The color coding is consistent across all modules and all metric types

steps.action: Verify that pagination or scrolling works if there are many modules or operations listed
steps.expectedResult: If pagination is implemented, page controls are visible and functional (Previous, Next, page numbers). If infinite scroll is used, additional data loads as the user scrolls down. The total count of items is displayed

postconditions: All UI elements are functional and interactive as expected
Page renders correctly across different viewport sizes
Sorting, filtering, and navigation controls work without errors

functional.title: Verify optimization recommendations include severity classification and are actionable
preconditions: User is logged in as Operations Manager on the Performance Benchmarking page

Multiple operations exceed their targets at varying degrees: one operation exceeds response time target by 200% (Critical), one exceeds throughput target by 30% (Medium), one exceeds error rate target by 10% (Low)
Optimization recommendations have been generated for all exceeding operations

steps.action: Navigate to the 'Optimization Recommendations' tab
steps.expectedResult: Recommendations are displayed in a list, sorted by severity (Critical first, then High, Medium, Low). Each recommendation has a visible severity badge/tag with color coding: Critical=Red, High=Orange, Medium=Yellow, Low=Blue

steps.action: Click on the Critical severity recommendation to view its full details
steps.expectedResult: The expanded recommendation shows: Module name, Operation name, Current metric value vs target, Percentage variance (200%), Severity: 'Critical', Detailed description of the issue, Recommended action steps (e.g., '1. Review database query execution plan, 2. Add index on frequently queried columns, 3. Implement query caching'), and Estimated improvement impact (e.g., 'Expected 65% reduction in response time, bringing it within target')

steps.action: Filter recommendations by severity by clicking on a 'Critical' filter chip or selecting 'Critical' from a severity dropdown
steps.expectedResult: Only Critical severity recommendations are displayed. The count updates to show the filtered number (e.g., 'Showing 1 of 3 recommendations'). A clear filter option is available

steps.action: Clear the severity filter and verify all recommendations are shown again
steps.expectedResult: All recommendations reappear in the list, sorted by severity. The count shows the total number of recommendations

steps.action: Verify that each recommendation's estimated improvement impact includes quantified metrics
steps.expectedResult: Every recommendation displays a specific estimated impact with numbers (e.g., '~150ms response time reduction', '20% throughput improvement', '0.5% error rate reduction'). No recommendation shows generic or vague impact descriptions

postconditions: All recommendations are properly classified by severity based on the degree of target threshold exceedance
Estimated improvement impacts are quantified and specific for each recommendation
Filtering and sorting of recommendations works correctly without data loss

userStoryObject.title: As Operations Manager, I want to perform module-level performance benchmarking and comparison to achieve identification of optimization opportunities across insurance operations
userStoryObject.description:
CONTEXT & BACKGROUND:
Benchmark reports must compare actual performance against targets for each insurance module, identifying the slowest operations and highest-impact optimization opportunities.

CURRENT STATE:
Manual or incomplete process today.

DESIRED STATE:
System supports the capability end-to-end.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
• Implement core logic and validation
• Expose API and UI
• Handle errors and edge cases
• Ensure data persistence and retrieval

USER INTERACTION FLOW:
1. User accesses the feature
2. User performs the required action
3. System processes the request
4. System displays results or confirmation
5. User verifies the outcome

TECHNICAL CONSIDERATIONS:
• Data source: application database
• Security: authentication and input validation
• Performance: meet SLA requirements

OUT OF SCOPE:
• Advanced customization (future story)

SUCCESS METRICS:
• Feature works per acceptance criteria
• No critical defects in production

**Persona:** Operations Manager

**Acceptance Criteria:**
1. Benchmark reports rank all insurance modules and top operations by response time, throughput, and error rate against defined targets
2. Optimization recommendations are generated for operations exceeding target thresholds with estimated improvement impact
3. Benchmark comparisons across environments (dev, staging, production) identify environment-specific performance variances
4. System handles the operation correctly and returns expected results for scenario 4
5. System handles the operation correctly and returns expected results for scenario 5
